void add_eas_records(const std::string& table_name,

  std::vector<std::optional<T>>> converted_records;

      s.SName = rec[1];

      c.CName = rec[1];
      c.Term = rec[2];

      Score sc;
      sc.ScID = rec[0];
      sc.SNo = rec[1];
      sc.CID = rec[2];

      m.MID = rec[0];
      m.MName = rec[1];
      converted_records.push_back(m);
    }

    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table: " + table_name);
  }

  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);
  for (const auto& obj : converted_records) {

  }
}

  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unsupported column type: " + col_name);
  }

  conn->insert(old_record);

// add+modify_eas_records.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "eas_common.hpp"
using namespace sqlgen;
using namespace sqlgen::literals;

namespace {

template <const std::string& _name>
struct GetStructType;

template <const std::string& _name>
struct GetStructType {
  using Type =
      typename rfl::named_tuple_t<std::conditional_t<_name == "Student", Student,
                                                     std::conditional_t<_name == "Course", Course,
                                                            std::conditional_t<_name == "Score", Score, Major>>>>::Type;
};

template <const std::string& _name>
using get_struct_type = typename GetStructType<_name>::Type;

}  // namespace

void add_eas_records(const std::string& _table_name,
                     const std::vector<std::tuple<std::string, std::string, std::optional<std::string>, std::optional<std::string>, std::optional<std::string>, std::optional<std::string>, std::optional<std::string>>>& _records) {
  using T = get_struct_type(_table_name);

  std::vector<std::optional<T>> converted_records;
  for (const auto& _record : _records) {
    T record;
    size_t i = 0;
    rfl::for_each(rfl::named_tuple_t<T>().fields(), [&](const auto& _field) {
      using FieldType = std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(_field)>::Type;
      if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, std::string>) {
        rfl::set(_field, std::get<i>(_record), record);
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, std::optional<std::string>) {
        rfl::set(_field, std::get<i>(_record), record);
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, int>) {
        if (std::get<i>(_record)) {
          rfl::set(_field, std::stoi(*std::get<i>(_record)), record);
        } else {
          rfl::set(_field, 0, record);
        }
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, uint32_t>) {
        if (std::get<i>(_record)) {
          rfl::set(_field, std::stoul(*std::get<i>(_record)), record);
        } else {
          rfl::set(_field, 0, record);
        }
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, double>) {
        if (std::get<i>(_record)) {
          rfl::set(_field, std::stod(*std::get<i>(_record)), record);
        } else {
          rfl::set(_field, 0.0, record);
        }
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<FieldType>, sqlgen::DateTime>) {
        if (std::get<i>(_record)) {
          rfl::set(_field, sqlgen::DateTime::from_string(*std::get<i>(_record)), record);
        } else {
          rfl::set(_field, sqlgen::DateTime{}, record);
        }
      }
      ++i;
    });
    converted_records.push_back(std::move(record));
  }

  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);
  for (const auto& _record : converted_records) {
    insert(conn, _record);
  }
}

void modify_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id,
                              const std::string& _col_name,
                              const std::optional<std::string>& _new_value) {
  using T = get_struct_type(_table_name);

  const auto fields = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>().fields();
  const auto pk_field = std::find_if(fields.begin(), fields.end(), [](const auto& _f) {
    return std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(_f)>::is_primary_key_v;
  });

  if (pk_field == fields.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("No primary key found for table: " + _table_name);
  }

  using PkType = std::remove_cvref_t<typename decltype(*pk_field)::Type>;
  PkType pk_value;
  if constexpr (std::is_same_v<PkType, std::string>) {
    pk_value = _record_id;
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<PkType, uint32_t>) {
    pk_value = std::stoul(_record_id);
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unsupported primary key type: " + _table_name);
  }

  const auto col_field = std::find_if(fields.begin(), fields.end(), [&](const auto& _f) {
    return _f.name() == _col_name;
  });

  if (col_field == fields.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Column not found: " + _col_name);
  }

  auto query = from<T>().where(eq(*pk_field, pk_value));
  auto res = sqlite::connect(db_path)->template read<std::vector<std::optional<T>>>>(query);
  if (res.value().empty()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Record not found: " + _record_id);
  }
  auto record = std::move(res.value()[0]);

  using FieldType = std::remove_cvref_t<typename decltype(*col_field)::Type>;
  if constexpr (std::is_same_v<FieldType, std::optional<std::string>) {
    rfl::set(*col_field, _new_value, record);
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<FieldType, int>) {
    if (!_new_value) {
      throw std::runtime_error("New value is required for integer column");
    }
    rfl::set(*col_field, std::stoi(*_new_value), record);
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<FieldType, uint32_t>) {
    if (!_new_value) {
      throw std::runtime_error("New value is required for uint32_t column");
    }
    rfl::set(*col_field, std::stoul(*_new_value), record);
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<FieldType, double>) {
    if (!_new_value) {
      throw std::runtime_error("New value is required for double column");
    }
    rfl::set(*col_field, std::stod(*_new_value), record);
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<FieldType, sqlgen::DateTime>) {
    if (!_new_value) {
      throw std::runtime_error("New value is required for DateTime column");
    }
    rfl::set(*col_field, sqlgen::DateTime::from_string(*_new_value), record);
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unsupported column type: " + _col_name);
  }

  sqlite::connect(db_path)->insert(record);
}

void delete_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id) {
  using T = get_struct_type(_table_name);

  const auto fields = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>().fields();
  const auto pk_field = std::find_if(fields.begin(), fields.end(), [](const auto& _f) {
    return std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(_f)>::is_primary_key_v;
  });

  if (pk_field == fields.end()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("No primary key found for table: " + _table_name);
  }

  using PkType = std::remove_cvref_t<typename decltype(*pk_field)::Type>;
  PkType pk_value;
  if constexpr (std::is_same_v<PkType, std::string>) {
    pk_value = _record_id;
  } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<PkType, uint32_t>) {
    pk_value = std::stoul(_record_id);
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unsupported primary key type: " + _table_name);
  }

  const auto query = delete_from<T>().where(eq(*pk_field, pk_value));
  sqlite::connect(db_path)->execute(query.str());
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <filesystem>

namespace batch_insert_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  float price;
  std::string category;
  int stock;
};

bool batch_insert_products(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
  const auto db_path = "test_db.sqlite";

  // Delete existing database file if it exists
  if (std::filesystem::exists(db_path)) {
    std::filesystem::remove(db_path);
  }

  try {
    // Create connection
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(db_path).value();

    // Create Product table
    const std::string create_table_sql = "CREATE TABLE Product ("
                                         "  product_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,"
                                         "  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,"
                                         "  price REAL NOT NULL,"
                                         "  category VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,"
                                         "  stock INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0"
                                         ");";
    conn->execute(create_table_sql);

    // Begin transaction
    conn->begin_transaction();

    // Batch insert products
    const auto num_inserted = conn->write(products);

    // Verify all products were inserted
    if (num_inserted != products.size()) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Partial insertion occurred");
    }

    // Commit transaction
    conn->commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Rollback on error
    try {
      conn->rollback();
    } catch (...) {
      // Ignore potential rollback errors
    }
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace batch_insert_products
</answer>

namespace batch_insert_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  float price;
  std::string category;
  int stock;
};

bool batch_insert_products(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
  const auto db_path = "test_db.sqlite";

  // Delete existing database file if it exists
  if (std::filesystem::exists(db_path)) {
    std::filesystem::remove(db_path);
  }

  try {
    // Create connection
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(db_path).value();

    // Create Product table
    const std::string create_table_sql = "CREATE TABLE Product ("
                                         "  product_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,"
                                         "  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,"
                                         "  price REAL NOT NULL,"
                                         "  category VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,"
                                         "  stock INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0"
                                         ");";
    conn->execute(create_table_sql);

    // Begin transaction
    conn->begin_transaction();

    // Batch insert products
    const auto num_inserted = conn->write(products);

    // Verify all products were inserted
    if (num_inserted != products.size()) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Partial insertion occurred");
    }

    // Commit transaction
    conn->commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Rollback on error
    try {
      conn->rollback();
    } catch (...) {
      // Ignore potential rollback errors
    }
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace batch_insert_products

<answer>```cpp
#include <filesystem>
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_design_eas_database {

struct Student {
  std::string SNo;
  std::string SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<std::string> Birthday;
  std::optional<int> Major;
  std::optional<float> Scorses = 0.0f;
  std::optional<std::string> Remark;
};

struct Course {
  int CID;
  std::string CName;
  int Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;
  float Credit = 0.0f;
};

struct Score {
  std::string SNo;
  int CID;
  std::optional<float> Score;
};

void design_eas_database() {
  const auto db_file = "test_db.sqlite";
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(db_file);

  // Create Student table
  auto student_table = sqlgen::create_table<Student>().transform([](auto& t) {
    t.columns[0].type = sqlgen::types::VarChar{.length = 10};
    t.columns[0].primary = true;
    t.columns[0].nullable = false;
    t.columns[1].nullable = false;
    t.columns[2].has_default = true;
    t.columns[2].default_value = "1";
    t.columns[5].has_default = true;
    t.columns[5].default_value = "0";
    return t;
  });

  // Create Course table
  auto course_table = sqlgen::create_table<Course>().transform([](auto& t) {
    t.columns[0].type = sqlgen::types::Integer{};
    t.columns[0].primary = true;
    t.columns[0].auto_incr = true;
    t.columns[0].nullable = false;
    t.columns[1].nullable = false;
    t.columns[2].has_default = true;
    t.columns[2].default_value = "1";
    return t;
  });

  // Create Score table
  auto score_table = sqlgen::create_table<Score>().transform([](auto& t) {
    t.columns[0].type = sqlgen::types::VarChar{.length = 10};
    t.columns[0].nullable = false;
    t.columns[1].type = sqlgen::types::Integer{};
    t.columns[1].nullable = false;
    t.constraints.emplace_back(sqlgen::PrimaryKeyConstraint{
        .columns = {"SNo", "CID"}
    });
    t.columns[0].references = sqlgen::types::Reference{
        .table = "Student",
        .column = "SNo"
    };
    t.columns[1].references = sqlgen::types::Reference{
        .table = "Course",
        .column = "CID"
    };
    return t;
  });

  // Execute the create table statements
  conn->execute(student_table);
  conn->execute(course_table);
  conn->execute(score_table);
}

}  // namespace test_design_eas_database
```</answer>

Alternatively, use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, but sqlgen's create_as_table may throw an exception if the table exists. So perhaps wrap the creation in a try-catch block. But the problem doesn't mention handling errors, so maybe just proceed.

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>
#include <sstream>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  // Create tables
  conn->create_as_table<User>();
  conn->create_as_table<Post>();
  
  const auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 1};
  const auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 31};
  
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT DISTINCT u.user_id, u.username
    FROM User u
    INNER JOIN Post p ON u.user_id = p.user_id
    WHERE p.created_at BETWEEN ? AND ?
  )";
  
  const auto prepared = conn->prepare(query);
  const auto rows = prepared(start_date, end_date);
  
  std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
  for (const auto& row : rows) {
    ActiveUser au;
    au.user_id = row.get<int>("u.user_id");
    au.username = row.get<std::string>("u.username");
    result.push_back(std::move(au));
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  conn->create_as_table<User>();
  conn->create_as_table<Post>();
  
  const auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 1};
  const auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 31};
  
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT DISTINCT u.user_id, u.username

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {

    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = conn->session();

            .student_count = student_count,

<answer>
```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_course_enrollment_stats.hpp"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = conn->session();

    // Create tables
    session.execute(
        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Course (course_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL);");
    session.execute(
        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Enrollment (enrollment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, course_id INTEGER REFERENCES Course(course_id), grade REAL NULL);");

    // Execute the query
    const std::string query = "SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) AS student_count, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade "
                              "FROM Course c "
                              "LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id "
                              "GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;";
    std::vector<std::tuple<std::string, int, std::optional<double>>> results;
    session.read(query, results.begin(), results.end());

    // Convert results to CourseStats
    std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
    for (const auto& [title, student_count, avg_grade] : results) {
        CourseStats cs{
            .title = title,
            .student_count = student_count,
            .avg_grade = avg_grade
        };
        stats.push_back(std::move(cs));
    }

    return stats;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (but problem doesn't mention error handling)
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```
</answer>

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

// ... struct definitions ...

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Customer>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Order>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<OrderItem>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Product>>());

  // Prepare the query
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT p.name, o.order_date, oi.quantity
    FROM Customer c
    JOIN "Order" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE c.customer_id = ?
    ORDER BY o.order_date DESC;
  )";

  // Execute the query with the customer_id parameter
  const auto results = conn.prepare_statement(query)
      .value()
      .read<int>(customer_id);

  // Process the results
  std::vector<PurchaseHistory> history;
  for (const auto& row : results) {
    PurchaseHistory ph{
        .product_name = row["name"].get<std::string>(),
        .order_date = row["order_date"].get<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S>>(),
        .quantity = row["quantity"].get<int>()};
    history.push_back(std::move(ph));
  }

  return history;
}

}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history

#endif

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen/sqlite/connect.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/to_container.hpp>

#include "query_daily_traffic_metrics.hpp"

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  const auto sql = R"(
    SELECT strftime('%Y-%m-%d', visit_time) as visit_date, 
           COUNT(*) as total_visits, 
           COUNT(DISTINCT url) as unique_pages
    FROM PageVisit
    GROUP BY visit_date
    ORDER BY visit_date;
  )";

  const auto execute = [](const auto& _conn) {
    return _conn->execute(sql);
  };

  const auto extract_results = [](const auto& _res) {
    return sqlgen::to_container<std::vector<DailyTraffic>>(_res);
  };

  try {
    return sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite")
        .and_then(execute)
        .and_then(extract_results)
        .value_or(std::vector<DailyTraffic>());
  } catch (...) {
    return std::vector<DailyTraffic>();
  }
}

}  // namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = session.select_from<Employee>("e1")
      .left_join<Employee>("e2", "e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id")
      .select("e1.name", "e2.name");
  auto results = query.execute();
  std::vector<EmployeeManager> result;
  for (const auto& row : results) {
    EmployeeManager entry;
    entry.employee = row[0];
    entry.manager = row[1]; // Automatically handles NULL as std::nullopt
    result.push_back(std::move(entry));
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy
</answer>
output.result:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = session.select_from<Employee>("e1")
      .left_join<Employee>("e2", "e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id")
      .select("e1.name", "e2.name");
  auto results = query.execute();
  std::vector<EmployeeManager> result;
  for (const auto& row : results) {
    EmployeeManager entry;
    entry.employee = row[0];
    entry.manager = row[1]; // Automatically handles NULL as std::nullopt
    result.push_back(std::move(entry));
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_high_value_orders {

std::vector<OrderView> query_high_value_orders() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  return conn->select_from<Order, Customer>(
      .on<Order::customer_id == Customer::customer_id>(),
      .where<Order::total_amount > 1000>(),
      .order_by<Order::total_amount, sqlgen::desc>(),
      .fields<Order::order_id, Customer::name, Order::total_amount, Order::order_date>()
  );
}

}  // namespace query_high_value_orders
```</answer>

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>();
    auto conditions = std::vector<sqlgen::Condition<Book>>();

    if (filters.title_keyword) {
        auto like_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
            rfl::field("title").like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%"));
        conditions.push_back(std::move(like_condition));
    }

    if (filters.min_year) {
        auto year_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
            rfl::field("year") >= *filters.min_year);
        conditions.push_back(std::move(year_condition));
    }

    if (filters.genre) {
        auto genre_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
            rfl::field("genre") == *filters.genre);
        conditions.push_back(std::move(genre_condition));
    }

    if (filters.max_price) {
        auto price_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
            rfl::field("price") <= *filters.max_price);
        conditions.push_back(std::move(price_condition));
    }

    // Combine all conditions with AND
    auto combined_condition = true;
    for (const auto& cond : conditions) {
        combined_condition = combined_condition && cond;
    }

    query = query.where(combined_condition);

    const auto result = conn->execute(query).transform(
        [](auto&& res) -> std::vector<Book> {
            return res;
        });

    if (!result) {
        return {};
    }

    return result.value();
}

That should handle all the dynamic conditions correctly.

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "query_search_books_by_criteria.hpp"

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& _filters) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>();
  auto conditions = std::vector<sqlgen::Condition<Book>>();

  if (_filters.title_keyword) {
    auto like_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("title").like("%" + *_filters.title_keyword + "%"));
    conditions.push_back(std::move(like_condition));
  }

  if (_filters.min_year) {
    auto year_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("year") >= *_filters.min_year);
    conditions.push_back(std::move(year_condition));
  }

  if (_filters.genre) {
    auto genre_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("genre") == *_filters.genre);
    conditions.push_back(std::move(genre_condition));
  }

  if (_filters.max_price) {
    auto price_condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("price") <= *_filters.max_price);
    conditions.push_back(std::move(price_condition));
  }

  auto combined_condition = true;
  for (const auto& cond : conditions) {
    combined_condition = combined_condition && cond;
  }

  query = query.where(combined_condition);

  const auto result = conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& _res) { return std::move(_res); });

  return result.value_or(std::vector<Book>());
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct AuditLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> log_id;
  std::string table_name;
  int record_id;
  std::string action;
  sqlgen::DateTime timestamp;
};

bool create_trigger_audit_log() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string trigger_sql = R"(
      CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS audit_product_update
      AFTER UPDATE ON Product
      BEGIN
          INSERT INTO AuditLog (table_name, record_id, action, timestamp)
          VALUES ('Product', OLD.product_id, 'UPDATE', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
      END;
    )";
    conn->execute(trigger_sql);
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log
```</answer>

The problem says to use the IN clause or batched updates. So using a single UPDATE statement with all the IDs in the IN clause is acceptable.

So the code should generate a query like:

UPDATE Document SET is_deleted = 1, deleted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE doc_id IN (1, 2, 3)

But with parameterized placeholders to prevent SQL injection.

In sqlgen, the in clause can take a vector and generate the appropriate number of placeholders.

So the code would be:

auto query = sqlgen::update<Document>()
    .set("is_deleted", 1)
    .set("deleted_at", sqlgen::current_timestamp())
    .where("doc_id", sqlgen::in(ids))
    .to_sql();

But then, when executing this query, the parameters (the ids) need to be passed. However, the to_sql() method returns the SQL string with placeholders, but the parameters are not included. So how to pass them?

Ah, here's the confusion. The ORM's update method may not handle the parameters automatically. So perhaps the correct approach is to use the dynamic general approach.

So, the code would be:

auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

std::string sql = "UPDATE Document SET is_deleted = 1, deleted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE doc_id IN ("
    + sqlgen::to_sql_placeholders(ids.size()) + ")";

auto query = conn->prepare(sql);

for (size_t i = 0; i < ids.size(); ++i) {
    query.bind(i + 1, ids[i]);
}

query.execute();

This way, the placeholders are replaced with the actual IDs.

But how to get the number of placeholders. The to_sql_placeholders(n) function returns a string like "?, ?, ?" for n=3.

Yes, that's correct.

So the code would generate the SQL with the correct number of placeholders, then bind each ID in the vector to the corresponding placeholder.

This approach ensures that the parameters are safely passed, preventing SQL injection.

So putting this into the function:

The function would:

- Check if the ids vector is empty. If so, return early (nothing to do).

- Connect to the database.

- Prepare the SQL statement with the IN clause.

- Bind each ID in the vector to the placeholders.

- Execute the query.

- Return true if successful, catch exceptions and return false.

So the code would look like:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace soft_delete_records {

struct Document {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> doc_id;
  std::string title;
  int is_deleted = 0;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> deleted_at;
};

bool soft_delete_records(const std::vector<int>& ids) {
  if (ids.empty()) {
    return true; // Nothing to delete
  }

  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    std::string sql = "UPDATE Document SET is_deleted = 1, deleted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE doc_id IN ("
        + sqlgen::to_sql_placeholders(ids.size()) + ")";

    auto query = conn->prepare(sql);

    for (size_t i = 0; i < ids.size(); ++i) {
      query.bind(i + 1, ids[i]);
    }

    query.execute();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Log the error if needed
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace soft_delete_records

<answer>
```cpp
// eas_common.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

using namespace sqlgen::literals;

struct Student {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>> SNo;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> Birthday;
  std::optional<uint32_t> Major;
  std::optional<double> Scorses = 0.0;
  std::optional<std::string> Remark;
};

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> CID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<50> CName;
  std::optional<int> Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;
  int Credit = 0;
};

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> ScID;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>, Student, "SNo"> SNo;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<uint32_t, Course, "CID"> CID;
  std::optional<double> Score;
};

struct Major {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t> MID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> MName;
};

const std::string db_path = "data/eas_database.db";

void design_eas_database_without_major() {
  std::filesystem::create_directories("data");
  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then([](auto&& conn) {
        return sqlgen::exec(conn, "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
      })
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Student> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Course> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Score> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}

void design_eas_database_with_major() {
  design_eas_database_without_major();

  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Major> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}
```

```cpp
// update_eas_table.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  design_eas_database_with_major();

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  // Add Grade column to Score table
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;").value();

  // Modify Age column in Student table to int allowing nulls
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student ALTER COLUMN Age TYPE INTEGER;").value();
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student ALTER COLUMN Age DROP NOT NULL;").value();

  // Drop Major table
  conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;").value();
}

} // namespace update_eas_table
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "upsert_user_profile.hpp"

namespace upsert_user_profile {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

bool upsert_user_profile(const UserProfile& _user) {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string sql =
        "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO UserProfile (user_id, bio, avatar_url) "
        "VALUES (?, ?, ?);";
    const auto stmt = conn->prepare(sql);

    stmt.bind(_user.user_id);
    stmt.bind(_user.bio);
    stmt.bind(_user.avatar_url);

    return stmt.execute();
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_user_profile
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  auto session = conn->session();

  const auto& A_ = session.get_table<A>();
  const auto& B_ = session.get_table<B>();

  auto query = session.selectFrom<A>()
      .fields(A_.name, B_.score)
      .join<B>(sqlgen::JoinType::inner_join, "b", [&](const auto& _a, const auto& _b) {
        return _a.id == _b.a_id;
      })
      .where([&](const auto& _a, const auto& _b) {
        return _b.score > 10 && _a.name.like("%x%");
      })
      .order_by(B_.score.desc())
      .limit(5);

  return query.to_sql();
}
}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
```</answer>

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data(
    const std::vector<Event>& _events) {
  try {
    // Connect to the SQLite database.
    sqlgen::SQLiteConn conn("test_db.sqlite");

    // Insert events into the database.
    conn.insert_or_write("Event", _events);

    // Define the SQL query based on the reference.
    const auto query = R"(
      SELECT 
        event_id,
        event_name,
        DAY(start_time) as start_day,
        MONTH(start_time) as start_month,
        YEAR(start_time) as start_year,
        HOUR(start_time) as start_hour,
        MINUTE(start_time) as start_minute,
        SECOND(start_time) as start_second,
        WEEKDAY(start_time) as start_weekday,
        UNIXEPOCH(start_time) as start_epoch,
        DAYS_BETWEEN(start_time, end_time) as duration_days
      FROM Event
      WHERE end_time IS NOT NULL;
    )";

    // Execute the query and process results.
    const auto result = conn.execute(query).transform([](auto cursor) {
      std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyses;
      while (cursor.next()) {
        TemporalAnalysis analysis;
        analysis.event_id = cursor.get<int>("event_id");
        analysis.event_name = cursor.get<std::string>("event_name");
        analysis.start_day = cursor.get<int>("start_day");
        analysis.start_month = cursor.get<int>("start_month");
        analysis.start_year = cursor.get<int>("start_year");
        analysis.start_hour = cursor.get<int>("start_hour");
        analysis.start_minute = cursor.get<int>("start_minute");
        analysis.start_second = cursor.get<int>("start_second");
        analysis.start_weekday = cursor.get<int>("start_weekday");
        analysis.start_epoch = cursor.get<int64_t>("start_epoch");
        analysis.duration_days = cursor.get<std::optional<double>>("duration_days");
        analyses.push_back(std::move(analysis));
      }
      return analyses;
    });

    // Handle potential errors.
    if (!result) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Query failed: " + std::string(result.error().what()));
    }

    return result.value();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Log the error message or handle it appropriately.
    std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data

import sqlgen
from sqlgen import Column, ForeignKey, Integer, String, DateTime, SQLiteConnector
from sqlgen import Session

class User(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    email = Column(String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    join_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    city = Column(String(50))

class Friendship(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id1 = Column(Integer, ForeignKey(User.user_id), primary_key=True)
    user_id2 = Column(Integer, ForeignKey(User.user_id), primary_key=True)
    friendship_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)

def analyze_social_network():
    session = Session.connect(SQLiteConnector, 'test_db.sqlite')
    
    # Create tables if they don't exist
    session.create_as(User, if_not_exists=True)
    session.create_as(Friendship, if_not_exists=True)
    
    # Query 5: Same city, not friends
    same_city_not_friends_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f 
        WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
           OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    );
    """
    recommendations = session.execute(same_city_not_friends_query).fetchall()
    
    session.close()
    
    return [{'user1_name': row[0], 'user2_name': row[1]} for row in recommendations]
```</answer>

import sqlgen
from sqlgen import Column, ForeignKey, Integer, String, DateTime, SQLiteConnector
from sqlgen import Session

class User(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    email = Column(String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    join_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    city = Column(String(50))

class Friendship(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id1 = Column(Integer, ForeignKey(User.user_id), primary_key=True)
    user_id2 = Column(Integer, ForeignKey(User.user_id), primary_key=True)
    friendship_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)

def analyze_social_network():
    session = Session.connect(SQLiteConnector, 'test_db.sqlite')
    
    # Create tables if they don't exist
    session.create_as(User, if_not_exists=True)
    session.create_as(Friendship, if_not_exists=True)
    
    # Query 5: Same city, not friends
    same_city_not_friends_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f 
        WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
           OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    );
    """
    recommendations = session.execute(same_city_not_friends_query).fetchall()
    
    session.close()
    
    return [{'user1_name': row[0], 'user2_name': row[1]} for row in recommendations]

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <string>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_conn("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto rows = conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& it) {
        return std::vector<std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>>{
          std::move(*it),
          while (it->next()) {
            std::move(*it)
          }};
      });

  return rows.transform([](auto&& vec) {
    std::vector<MathAnalysis> result;
    for (const auto& row : vec) {
      MathAnalysis ma;
      ma.product_id = std::stoi(row[0]);
      ma.name = row[1];
      ma.abs_price = std::stod(row[2]);
      ma.ceil_price = std::stod(row[3]);
      ma.floor_price = std::stod(row[4]);
      ma.rounded_price = std::stod(row[5]);
      ma.exp_discount = std::stod(row[6]);
      ma.ln_price = std::stod(row[7]);
      ma.log2_quantity = std::stod(row[8]);
      ma.sin_discount = std::stod(row[9]);
      ma.cos_discount = std::stod(row[10]);
      ma.tan_discount = std::stod(row[11]);
      ma.sqrt_quantity = std::stod(row[12]);
      result.push_back(std::move(ma));
    }
    return result;
  }).value_or(std::vector<MathAnalysis>());
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "sqlgen.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.h"

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_conn("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate / 100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto to_math_analysis = [](const std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>& _row) -> MathAnalysis {
    return MathAnalysis{
        .product_id = std::stoi(_row[0]),
        .name = _row[1],
        .abs_price = std::stod(_row[2]),
        .ceil_price = std::stod(_row[3]),
        .floor_price = std::stod(_row[4]),
        .rounded_price = std::stod(_row[5]),
        .exp_discount = std::stod(_row[6]),
        .ln_price = std::stod(_row[7]),
        .log2_quantity = std::stod(_row[8]),
        .sin_discount = std::stod(_row[9]),
        .cos_discount = std::stod(_row[10]),
        .tan_discount = std::stod(_row[11]),
        .sqrt_quantity = std::stod(_row[12])};
  };

  return conn->read<vector<MathAnalysis>>(query, to_math_analysis);
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

struct OrderMetrics {
  int item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;
  double final_total;
  double unit_cost;
  int quantity_group;
  double estimated_profit;
};

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_connection("test_db.sqlite");
  conn->execute("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS OrderItem;");
  conn->execute("CREATE TABLE OrderItem (
      item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      unit_price REAL NOT NULL,
      quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
      discount_rate REAL,
      tax_rate REAL
  );");
  conn->write(items);

  const std::string query = R"(
    SELECT 
      item_id,
      order_id,
      product_id,
      unit_price,
      quantity,
      COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0) as discount_rate,
      COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0) as tax_rate,
      (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
      (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100)) as discounted_total,
      (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0)/100)) as final_total,
      (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
      (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
      (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
    FROM OrderItem;
  )";

  std::vector<OrderMetrics> results;
  conn->execute(query).read_to(results);

  return results;
}
}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {

  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;

  double unit_cost;

  double estimated_profit;
};

#pragma once
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/connect.hpp"

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Customer>().where("customer_id = :id").param("id", id);
  const auto res = conn->template read<std::vector<Customer>>(query);
  if (!res) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to execute query: " +
                             std::string(res.error().what()));
  }
  if (res->size() != 1) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Expected exactly one result, got " +
                             std::to_string(res->size()));
  }
  return std::move((*res)[0]);
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "join_orders_customers_payments.hpp"

namespace join_orders_customers_payments {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  int customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
};

struct Payment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> payment_id;
  int order_id;
  double amount;
};

struct Consolidated {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer_name;
  double amount;
};

std::vector<Consolidated> join_orders_customers_payments() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = sqlgen::make_session(conn);

  const auto query = session->select_from<Customer, sqlgen::Alias<"c">()
      .fields("o.order_id", "c.name", "p.amount")
      .inner_join<Order, sqlgen::Alias<"o">(), "c.customer_id = o.customer_id")
      .inner_join<Payment, sqlgen::Alias<"p">(), "o.order_id = p.order_id")
      .order_by("p.amount DESC")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<Consolidated> result;
        for (const auto& res : range) {
          Consolidated entry;
          entry.order_id = res.get<int>("o.order_id");
          entry.customer_name = res.get<std::string>("c.name");
          entry.amount = res.get<double>("p.amount");
          result.emplace_back(std::move(entry));
        }
        return result;
      });

  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace join_orders_customers_payments
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto session = sqlgen::make_session<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Replace "foo" with "bar" in all notes
  session.execute("UPDATE Notes SET content = REPLACE(content, 'foo', 'bar')");
  
  // Fetch updated notes to verify changes
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  session.select_from<Note>().transform([&](auto range) {
    for (const auto& note : range) {
      updated_notes.emplace_back(
          .note_id = note.note_id,
          .updated_content = note.content);
    }
  });
  
  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Start transaction

  for (const auto& item : items) {

  }

  // Commit the transaction

  // Prepare the select query
  const int threshold = 50;
  auto query = conn->select_from<Inventory>("item_id, name, quantity, location")

      .order_by("quantity DESC");

  // Execute the query and get results
  std::vector<Inventory> results;
  query >> results;

  return !results.empty();
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace cascade_delete_demo {

struct Parent {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> parent_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Child {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> child_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Parent, "parent_id"> parent_id;
  std::string note;
};

bool cascade_delete_demo() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConn("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;"); // Enable foreign key support

    // Create tables
    conn.create_table<Parent>();
    conn.create_table<Child>());

    // Insert parent and child records
    Parent parent{.parent_id = 1, .name = "Test Parent"};
    conn.insert(parent);

    Child child{.child_id = 1, .parent_id = 1, .note = "Test Note"};
    conn.insert(child);

    // Delete parent and check if child is cascaded
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM Parent WHERE parent_id = 1;");
    const auto children = conn.select_from<Child>().where(Child::parent_id == 1).execute();

    return children.empty();
  } catch (...) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace cascade_delete_demo

namespace column_is_null_checks {

        columns.profile_id, columns.display_name)

    }
    return results;
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <rfl.hpp>

namespace update_set_operations {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
  std::string status;
};

const auto Account_ = rfl::named_tuple_t<Account>::names;

bool update_set_operations() {
  try {
    auto connector = sqlgen::SQLiteConnector("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = connector.connect();

    // Create the Account table
    session.createAs<Account>();

    // Insert initial data
    Account initial_account{.account_id = 1, .balance = 100.0, .status = "active"};
    session.insert(initial_account);

    // Update balance and status
    session.update<Account>()
        .where(Account_.account_id == 1)
        .set(Account_.balance = 200.0)
        .set(Account_.status = "inactive")
        .execute();

    // Verify the update
    auto updated_account = session.selectFrom<Account>().where(Account_.account_id == 1).value();

    return updated_account.balance == 200.0 && updated_account.status == "inactive";
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace update_set_operations
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace select_from_read_to_vector {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  int pages;
};

std::vector<Book> select_from_read_to_vector() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("books.db");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>().order_by(sqlgen::col("pages").desc()).to<std::vector<Book>>();
  return conn->execute(query);
}

}  // namespace select_from_read_to_vector
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace concat_function {

struct Person {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
};

struct FullName {
  std::string full_name;
};

inline auto concat_function() {
    const auto& p = Person{};
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from(p)
        .columns(sqlgen::CONCAT(p.first_name, " ", p.last_name).as("full_name"));
    return query.str();
}

}  // namespace concat_function
```
</answer>

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace get_top_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Date sale_date;
};

struct TopProduct {
  int product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) {
    return {};
  }

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>("p")
      .join<Sales>("s", "p.product_id = s.product_id")
      .select(p.product_id)
      .select(p.product_name)
      .select(p.category)
      .select(sqlgen::sum(p.price * s.quantity).as("total_sales"))
      .group_by(p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category)
      .order_by(sqlgen::col("total_sales").desc())
      .limit(static_cast<int>(limit_count));

  const auto res = conn->execute(query)
      .and_then([](auto& _stmt) {
        return _stmt.read_to<std::vector<TopProduct>>();
      });

  return res.value_or(std::vector<TopProduct>());
}
}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include "sqlgen/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"

namespace order_by_descending {

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int points;
};

inline std::string order_by_descending() {
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Score>().order_by(Score::points.desc());

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) return false;

  auto txn = begin_transaction(conn).transform([](auto&& _conn) { return _conn; });
  if (!txn) return false;

  const auto update_sql = "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?";
  auto update_stmt = txn->prepare_statement(update_sql);

  for (const auto& [product_id, quantity] : received) {
    auto res = update_stmt->execute(quantity, product_id);
    if (!res) {
      txn->rollback();
      return false;
    }
  }

  return commit(std::move(txn)).value_or(false);
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) return false;

  auto txn = begin_transaction(conn).transform([](auto&& _conn) { return _conn; });
  if (!txn) return false;

  const auto update_sql = "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?";
  auto update_stmt = txn->prepare_statement(update_sql);

  for (const auto& [product_id, quantity] : received) {
    auto res = update_stmt->execute(quantity, product_id);
    if (!res) {
      txn->rollback();
      return false;
    }
  }

  return commit(std::move(txn)).value_or(false);
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "create_view_order_summary.hpp"

namespace create_view_order_summary {

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");

  const std::string create_view_sql =
      "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
      "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) as total "
      "FROM Order o "
      "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
      "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;";
  conn->execute(create_view_sql);

  const auto query_result = sqlgen::select_from<OrderSummary>(conn);

  return query_result;
}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto sales = session.table<Sales>();

  const auto query = sales.select(
      sales.sale_time.strftime("%w").cast<int>().as("weekday"),
      sqlgen::sum(sales.quantity).as("total_quantity")
    )
    .group_by("weekday");

  const auto result = query.exec();

  std::vector<WeekdayStats> stats;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    WeekdayStats ws;
    ws.weekday = row["weekday"].as<int>();
    ws.total_quantity = row["total_quantity"].as<int>();
    stats.push_back(ws);
  }

  return stats;
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis

            sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::length(Post::content)).as("avg_length")

      s.post_count = row.get<int>("post_count");

      stats.push_back(std::move(s));
    }
    return stats;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {

  }
}

namespace group_by_with_aggregations {

struct User {

};

  int user_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct Stats {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  int post_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_length;
};

std::vector<Stats> group_by_with_aggregations() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session::make(std::move(conn));

    auto query = session.select_from<User>()
        .left_join<Post, User::user_id == Post::user_id>("p")
        .fields(
            User::user_id,
            User::name,
            sqlgen::count<Post::post_id>().as("post_count"),
            sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::length(Post::content)).as("avg_length")
        )
        .group_by(User::user_id, User::name)
        .to_sql();

    auto res = session.execute(query);
    if (!res) {
      return {};
    }

    std::vector<Stats> stats;
    for (const auto& row : *res) {
      Stats s;
      s.user_id = row.get<int>("user_id");
      s.name = row.get<std::string>("name");
      s.post_count = row.get<int>("post_count");
      s.avg_length = row.get<std::optional<double>>("avg_length");

}

            sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::length(Post::content)).as("avg_length")

Then, the to_sql function converts this to the SQL string.

Putting it all together, the code should look like this.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_table_as_select {

struct Source {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int value;
};

struct Target {
  int value;
};

inline auto create_table_as_select() {
    sqlite::Connection conn;
    auto select_from = sqlgen::SelectFrom<Source>{
        .where = sqlgen::columns<Source>().value > 100,
        .fields = {sqlgen::columns<Source>().value}
    };
    auto query = conn.create_as<Target>(select_from, "Target");
    return conn.to_sql(query);
}

}  // namespace create_table_as_select
```</answer>
